query_normalized: llmxive follow-up: extending "multi-lcb: extending livecodebench to multiple programming languages" computer science
rationale: This paper satisfies criteria (b) and (f) as it addresses the multilingual evaluation domain and the specific mechanism of linguistic bias in LLM benchmarks, which is a critical component of extending LiveCodeBench to multiple languages. It provides the necessary canonical discussion on why simple averaging fails in multilingual settings, a foundational concern for any work extending LiveCodeBench to new languages.
